_comment_hooks: Web Design OS uses a PostToolUse hook to remind Claude to refresh the dashboard whenever any client's project-state.md is edited. The hook emits a system reminder the next time Claude responds, so the designer doesn't have to manually say 'show my dashboard' after finishing a task. If this hook isn't firing as expected, comment it out and run 'show my dashboard' manually after each state change.

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'project-state.md'; then echo 'DASHBOARD_DIRTY=1' > /tmp/wdos-dashboard-dirty 2>/dev/null; echo '<system-reminder>A client project-state.md was just edited. The dashboard is out of date. Run workflow_dashboard.md to regenerate dashboard.html, the affected client workspace.html, and rendered resource HTML files before continuing.</system-reminder>'; fi

_comment_skills: Web Design OS skills auto-load from .claude/skills/ based on description matching. No manual invocation needed.
